Radiographs of the hand reveal a triangular ossification center at the base of the middle finger's proximal phalanx, accompanied by drumstick-shaped widening of the fingertips.  
The patient's hand radiograph demonstrates a characteristically triangular epiphysis of the third proximal phalanx and clubbed terminal phalanges.  
A skeletal survey shows a triangular proximal phalangeal epiphysis of the third digit, with concurrent drumstick deformity of the distal phalanges.  
Examination of the hand skeleton identifies a triangular epiphyseal shape in the proximal phalanx of the middle finger, along with drumstick terminal phalanges.  
Imaging reveals co-existing abnormalities: a triangular ossification center at the base of the third finger's proximal phalanx and a drumstick morphology of the distal phalanges.  
The radiographic phenotype includes a triangular epiphysis of the proximal phalanx of the third digit and drumstick-shaped terminal phalanges.
